---
title: "Hess's Law, Gibbs Free Energy, and Equilibrium Thermodynamics"
author: "Converted from Handwritten PDF"
date: today
lang: en
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    keep-tex: true
    number-sections: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
        \usepackage{fontspec}
        \usepackage{hyperref}
        \hypersetup{pdfauthor={Converted from PDF}, pdftitle={Hess's Law, Gibbs Free Energy, and Equilibrium Thermodynamics}, pdfsubject={Handwritten Notes}, pdfkeywords={notes, handwritten}, colorlinks=true, bookmarks=true, bookmarksopen=true, bookmarksnumbered=true}
    pdf-engine: xelatex
  docx:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

::: {.content-section}

# Physical Chemistry â€“ Study Sheet

## Hess's Law

$$\Delta H^\circ_{rxn} = \sum \Delta H^\circ_f \text{ (products)} - \sum \Delta H^\circ_f \text{ (reactants)}$$

- $\Delta H^\circ_f$ of any element in standard state $= 0$
- Flip reaction $\rightarrow$ flip sign of $\Delta H^\circ$
- Scale reaction by $n$ $\rightarrow$ scale $\Delta H^\circ$ by $n$

## Standard Entropy Change

$$\Delta S^\circ_{rxn} = \sum S^\circ \text{ (products)} - \sum S^\circ \text{ (reactants)}$$

- Units: $\text{J mol}^{-1} \text{K}^{-1}$ $\leftarrow$ watch the J vs kJ mismatch!
- More moles of gas $\Rightarrow$ $\Delta S^\circ > 0$ (usually)

## Gibbs Free Energy

**Standard Conditions (298 K):**

$$\Delta G^\circ = \Delta H^\circ - T\Delta S^\circ$$

**Non-Standard Conditions:**

$$\Delta G = \Delta G^\circ + RT \ln Q$$

$Q \equiv$ reaction quotient

| $\Delta G$ | Meaning |
|------------|---------|
| $< 0$ | spontaneous |
| $= 0$ | equilibrium |
| $> 0$ | non-spontaneous |

:::

::: {.content-section}

# Equilibrium Constant

$$\Delta GÂ° = -RT \ln K$$

$$\therefore K = e^{-\Delta GÂ°/RT}$$

- $R = 8.314 \text{ J mol}^{-1} \text{K}^{-1}$
- $T$ must be in Kelvin
- $K > 1$: products favored; $K < 1$: reactants favored

---

# Van't Hoff Equation

$$\ln \frac{K_2}{K_1} = -\frac{\Delta HÂ°}{R}\left(\frac{1}{T_2} - \frac{1}{T_1}\right)$$

## La Chatelier Shortcut:

| Reaction Type | Raise T | Lower T |
|---|---|---|
| Exothermic ($\Delta HÂ° < 0$) | K decreases | K increases |
| Endothermic ($\Delta HÂ° > 0$) | K increases | K decreases |

---

# Unit Checklist

| Symbol | Units |
|---|---|
| $\Delta HÂ°$ | $\text{kJ mol}^{-1}$ |
| $\Delta SÂ°$ | $\text{J mol}^{-1} \text{K}^{-1}$ (convert to kJ before combining with $\Delta HÂ°$) |
| $\Delta GÂ°$ | $\text{kJ mol}^{-1}$ |
| $R$ (with $\Delta GÂ°$) | $8.314 \times 10^{-3} \text{ kJ mol}^{-1} \text{K}^{-1}$ or convert $\Delta GÂ°$ to J |
| $T$ | Kelvin |

:::

::: {.content-section}

![Diagram or drawing from handwritten notes](figure_3.png){width=100%}

# Workflow

{{FIGURE: Flowchart diagram showing the thermodynamic workflow described below}}

---

**Top Level Inputs:**

| $\Delta H^\circ_f$ tables std enthalpies of formation | $S^\circ$ tables std molar entropies |
|---|---|

---

**Second Level (Computations):**

- **Hess's Law** â€” compute $\Delta H^\circ_{rxn}$
- **Entropy Sum** â€” compute $\Delta S^\circ_{rxn}$

---

**Third Level (Gibbs std):**

$$\Delta G^\circ = \Delta H^\circ - T\Delta S^\circ \ (298)$$

---

**Fourth Level (Three Branches):**

**Left branch:**
Non-std $\Delta G$
$$\Delta G = \Delta G^\circ + RT\ln Q$$

**Middle branch:**
Equilibrium $K$
$$K = e^{-\Delta G^\circ/RT}$$

**Right branch:**
Van Hoff
$K$ shifts w $T$

---

**Bottom Level:**

**Spontaneity & direction**

$$\Delta G < 0 \rightarrow \text{forward favored}$$

:::
